This creature is an African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae), distinguishable by its vibrant green wings and exceptionally long trailing hindwing tails.
As adults, these moths possess no working mouthparts and cannot feed, relying entirely on energy stored during their larval stage.
Their adult lifespan is very short, typically lasting less than a week, during which their primary purpose is to mate and lay eggs.
The large, moon-like eyespots on their wings serve as a defense mechanism, resembling the eyes of a much larger animal to deter potential predators.
